text: Never provision this project's backend objects (tables, RPCs, edge functions) inside an existing/unrelated Supabase project, even prefixed and flagged reversible — the owner ruled the 'insurance' project strictly off-limits and required a full teardown on 2026-07-10. If no dedicated project slot exists, STOP and ask the owner (free a slot / upgrade); never fall back to a shared project. Any resource-scope decision outside this repo needs explicit owner approval BEFORE proceeding.

The Claude Code Remote MCP server registers as BOTH mcp__Claude_Code_Remote__* and mcp__claude-code-remote__* depending on surface — allowlist rules must cover both spellings (PR #50). CONFIRMED by owner-witnessed experiment (2026-07-13, this repo): permissions.allow is SNAPSHOTTED at session start — with the exactly-matching rule present on disk mid-session, a send_later call still raised the permission dialog. /refresh-repo (or pulling main) does NOT reload harness permissions; only sessions started after the allowlist merge run prompt-free. Ride out running sessions with 'Allow once'.

Migrating a scheduled GitHub-Actions data pipeline to Supabase edge functions worked cleanly as: (0) prove the pattern on ONE feed (desk-maps) before committing the plan, (A) port public feeds behind the SAME payload shapes with the committed snapshots as a transition fallback, (B) move cron jobs to pg_cron+Vault (headers resolved at run time so migrations stay literal-free; dual slots because upstream statements roll overnight), (C) delete the pipeline and the fallback in one PR only after B verified live. Verify every function server-side via SELECT net.http_post(...) then read net._http_response — no browser needed, works from any SQL-capable session, and proves the exact call pg_cron will make (same Vault header assembly). Edge wall-clock forces honest not-ready exits over long polling; the second cron slot IS the retry.

When explaining how anything works to the owner (data flows, architecture, processes, options), lead with a simple table — one row per component, plain-language columns like what / where it comes from / when it updates / how it reaches you — then at most two takeaway sentences. No jargon inside cells; drill into mechanism only when asked. Confirmed 2026-07-13 after the post-migration data-flow explanation: the prose version was fine, the tabular rewrite is what the owner wants 'for understanding in general'.

When implementing something with an obvious reference product or expectation (e.g. 'Russell 2000 map' = all ~2000 names, like finviz), never silently deliver a reduced scope for craft reasons (legibility, quota, performance). Either build the full expected thing, or STOP and tell the owner the trade-off BEFORE shipping ('finviz shows all 2000 as unlabeled mosaic; I can match that or cap at 300 readable tiles — which?'). Owner ruling 2026-07-14 after the r2k map shipped with a top-300 cap: 'if you can accomplish something that is literally expected, you need to let me know.' A caption disclosing the cut is not enough — the choice belongs to the owner.

For aesthetic/sizing changes (widget dimensions, panel layout, spacing), show the owner a mock of the proposed look BEFORE shipping, and make one decisive larger change rather than tiny pixel-nudging increments — the owner iterated ~10 rounds on Market-widget sizing (PRs #84-#94 era) and got visibly frustrated with incrementalism. Corollary: third-party vendor iframes (TradingView/FRED) render blank in the sandbox because outbound CONNECT to their hosts is blocked, so a local Playwright render cannot show real widget content — measure target sizes from the owner's screenshots instead of a local render.

When the owner explicitly says 'merge' / 'merge asap' for a specific PR, that is an express-merge order: do not hold that PR for in-flight CI — mark it ready and squash-merge immediately, provided the diff is in the auto-merge-on-green class (frontend/docs/tests/config, fully undone by git revert) and carries no hold-class content (secrets, elevated-secret workflow config, Supabase backend — those holds are never bypassed). Let CI finish afterward and treat any late failure as the immediate top follow-up (fix-forward or revert-first). Owner ruling 2026-07-16 ('Next time when I say merge, you can cut the testing for that one time short so I can make quicker progress with the dashboard'), first applied to PR #98. Express merge trades the CI wait only — local verification before pushing is still expected.
